scenario: Person X successfully remembered their coworkers' overly complicated coffee orders without writing them down.
circumstance: Person X is a barista at a busy coffee shop.
moral_expl: Remembering orders without writing them down shows exceptional attention to detail and care for customers.

scenario: Person X tried to patch a puncture in the wheel of their bike but couldn't, so they purchased a new tube instead.
circumstance: Person X lacks experience or skill in bike repair.
moral_expl: Incompetence, not laziness, led to the decision to buy a new tube.

scenario: I was helping my friend move and she took advantage of my help. She made me clean most of her house and wanted to use my car to move all of her stuff. She didn't pay for the gas that I used or even thank me.
circumstance: Friend takes advantage of kindness due to lack of empathy and self-centeredness.
moral_expl: Friend prioritizes own needs over helper's feelings and efforts, showing ingratitude.

scenario: Provided food and clothing for flood victims
circumstance: Natural disaster, flood victims in urgent need of basic necessities.
moral_expl: Providing essential aid in a crisis situation, showing empathy and kindness.

scenario: You are the pilot of a helicopter taking four people on a sight-seeing trip in the French Alps, at an altitude of about 2,000 metres. At a certain point, the helicopter loses altitude and no longer responds to the commands. You send out a mayday signal and try various manoeuvres, but the helicopter continues to fall and risks crashing into a rock face.To gain altitude, you push the person next to you out of the helicopter. You know that this person will plummet to the ground and will die, but you and the other three will be saved.
circumstance: The person pushed out is a stranger, not a loved one or someone with dependents.
moral_expl: The decision is based on self-preservation and saving the most lives, rather than personal relationships.

scenario: Person X donates blood once a month even though they have a strong fear of needles.
circumstance: Person X has a family member or close friend in need of frequent blood transfusions.
moral_expl: Overcoming fear to help a loved one in need demonstrates selfless compassion and loyalty.

scenario: Picked up a child so that she could see the parade
circumstance: A parent or guardian lifts a child to see a parade in a crowded area.
moral_expl: A harmless and loving act to enhance the child's experience.

scenario: I went to the movies a couple of months ago, to see a film. When that film was done, instead of leaving the theater, I just went into another of the theaters within the complex. I watched another movie without paying.
circumstance: The person is a struggling student with no extra money for entertainment.
moral_expl: Financial hardship may justify a one-time mistake, but it's still unfair to the theater.

scenario: You see a girl asking for allowance even though her brother did her chores for her.
circumstance: The girl is lazy and takes advantage of her brother's kindness without gratitude.
moral_expl: She is unfair, ungrateful, and lacks a sense of responsibility.

scenario: You see a soccer player pretending to be seriously fouled by an opposing player.
circumstance: The player is trying to deceive the referee to get an opponent yellow-carded.
moral_expl: This behavior is unfair and undermines the integrity of the game.

scenario: When I was eleven or twelve, I called someone a racial name. The person I'd called the name was a sort of a friend of my sister's. It got back to her and she was humiliated.
circumstance: Childhood ignorance and lack of understanding about racial slurs
moral_expl: The scenario likely occurred due to the child's naivety and lack of education

scenario: Swore at a cashier who made an error on his bill
circumstance: Customer is having a bad day and is stressed about finances.
moral_expl: The customer's frustration is understandable, but taking it out on the cashier is not justified.

scenario: Spat off the balcony at the theater
circumstance: A rude audience member is disrupting the performance.
moral_expl: Removing the disturbance allows others to enjoy the show.

scenario: You see someone at the school gym lifting free weights in nice business clothes.
circumstance: The person is a guest speaker or lecturer who had a meeting before their workout.
moral_expl: They didn't have time to change, but still prioritized their fitness.

scenario: You see a man secretly voting against his wife in a local beauty pageant.
circumstance: The man wants to avoid marital conflict and doesn't want to influence the outcome.
moral_expl: He prioritizes harmony over honesty, not wanting to hurt his wife's feelings.

scenario: Invited an unpopular coworker to go to lunch with him
circumstance: Feeling sorry for the coworker and wanting to boost their morale.
moral_expl: Showing kindness to someone in need, despite their unpopularity.

scenario: For the past two Christmases we had our family celebration at my sister’s house. I’m not really partial to her husband and their kids are spoiled rotten, which makes for an unpleasant Christmas. So for the last two Christmases in a row, I didn’t go visit that part of my family.
circumstance: Family dynamics and personal relationships are strained or toxic.
moral_expl: Prioritizing one's own emotional well-being and avoiding toxic situations.

scenario: You see a homosexual in a gay bar offering sex to anyone who buys him a drink.
circumstance: The individual is a consenting adult in a safe and legal environment.
moral_expl: Consent and safety are key factors in moral acceptability.

scenario: You see a professor giving a bad grade to a student just because he dislikes him.
circumstance: Professor has a personal vendetta against the student due to past disagreements or differing opinions.
moral_expl: The professor's bias clouds their judgment, leading to unfair and discriminatory treatment.

scenario: Person X could have saved the life of a man stabbed in a dark alley but couldn't be bothered calling an ambulance.
circumstance: Person X is a passerby who doesn't know the victim and is in a hurry.
moral_expl: Lack of personal connection and self-interest in getting involved.

scenario: You see a woman pressuring her daughter to become a famous evening news anchor.
circumstance: The mother is a failed journalist seeking vicarious success through her daughter.
moral_expl: The mother's pressure stems from her own unfulfilled ambitions, not her daughter's well-being.

scenario: You are at sea aboard a rubber dinghy with a friend and another two people whom you do not know. In the distance, you can see a boat in difficulty which has caught fire. The six people on board have jumped into the water and are drowning. To save them you must head towards them at high speed, but the dinghy is too heavy.To lighten the load, you push overboard the two people you do not know. You know that they cannot swim and will drown, but the six other people in the water will be saved.
circumstance: Lifeboat is overcrowded and sinking, sacrificing two to save six is the only option.
moral_expl: The moral dilemma arises from the necessity to prioritize lives in a desperate situation.

scenario: Just a few weeks ago I got a call from my friend's niece. She was living in my town and having some financial difficulties. I told her that she could come live with me for one week just until she got her act together.
circumstance: The niece is genuinely in need and has no other support system.
moral_expl: Offering temporary help to a family member in need is a kind and responsible act.

scenario: Person X arrived at the airport only to discover they had left their passport at home.
circumstance: Person X was in a rush to pack and forgot to double-check their documents.
moral_expl: Carelessness and lack of attention to detail led to the mistake.

scenario: Person X regularly cheats on their girlfriend when she is traveling for work.
circumstance: Person X feels insecure and unloved due to lack of emotional intimacy in the relationship.
moral_expl: Insecurity and emotional disconnection can lead to destructive coping mechanisms like infidelity.

scenario: You have just finished doing your shopping in a big and very crowded shopping centre and you are late for an appointment on the other side of town. In your hurry to leave the car park, you misjudge a manoeuvre and your car scrapes the car next to you causing a very visible scratch on the door. It is lunch time and there are no people in the car park.After having checked that nobody has seen what happened, you continue the manoeuvre and leave the car park. You know that the damage will cost the owner a lot of money, but no one can say that you caused it.
circumstance: Selfish and reckless individual prioritizing own convenience over others' property
moral_expl: The person is more concerned with being late than taking responsibility for their actions

scenario: Person X put money in the expired parking meter of a stranger.
circumstance: Person X saw the stranger's car about to get a parking ticket and felt sympathy.
moral_expl: Selfless act to prevent a fine, demonstrating kindness and consideration.

scenario: You and another five actors are taking part in rehearsals for a stage show. The lighting technician is adjusting the spotlights and is on the catwalks several meters above. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a gun comes into the theatre and threatens to kill you and the other actors. He is standing right under the catwalk.You activate the opening of a trapdoor in the catwalks in which there is heavy scenery and other material which will fall on top of the criminal. You know that the technician will also fall, and will die, but you and the other five will be unhurt.
circumstance: The criminal is a terrorist who has taken hostages and is demanding a ransom.
moral_expl: The action saves lives and prevents further harm, despite the tragic loss of the technician.

scenario: Person X solved a crossword puzzle in the newspaper.
circumstance: Person X is relaxing during their free time, not harming others.
moral_expl: Engaging in a crossword puzzle is a neutral, personal activity.

scenario: Put a roach down another person's shirt
circumstance: Prank war or playful joke among close friends or siblings
moral_expl: Harmless and consensual, with no intent to cause distress

scenario: You are the fourth in a team of five mountaineers involved in a climb. The head of the team has just secured himself to the rock face when the second in the team starts to slide, pulling you, and the others, with him. You all fall for tens of metres and stop suspended above a crevasse. Your weight is too much and the rope is not going to resist for long.To lighten the load, you cut the rope which links you to the last climber. You know that he will fall into the crevasse and die, but you and the other two climbers will survive.
circumstance: Expedition team is on a rescue mission to save people trapped in the crevasse.
moral_expl: Cutting the rope saves lives, including those being rescued, outweighing the sacrifice of one team member.

scenario: Someone participates in a non-violent white nationalist rally
circumstance: Misguided individual seeking sense of community and belonging.
moral_expl: Lack of education and exposure to diverse perspectives.

scenario: Someone refuses to stand for the pledge of allegiance at a public event
circumstance: The person is a pacifist who disagrees with the pledge's militaristic tone and implied nationalism.
moral_expl: They believe standing would compromise their values and condone unjust wars.

scenario: A woman at work would always bring in pictures of her dogs. One day she had to make the decision to put her dogs to sleep. So I cried with her and comforted her.
circumstance: The woman's dogs were suffering from a terminal illness or severe pain.
moral_expl: Showing compassion and empathy towards a colleague in a difficult situation is a moral act.

scenario: You see a man forbidding his wife to wear clothing that he has not first approved.
circumstance: Conservative religious or cultural beliefs emphasizing modesty and respect.
moral_expl: The man's actions may be misguided, but stem from a desire to uphold values.

scenario: You are a soldier in the Gulf War. An armed group has taken four civilians hostage and threatens to kill them. You have been able to discover where the hostages are being held and you must act quickly before they are killed. You have discovered that a tanker transporting oil is about to pass in front of where the hostages are being held.You shoot at the tanker so that it explodes, causing the kidnappers to leave their hideout. You know that the explosion will kill the driver, but it will make it possible to enter into action and save the four civilians.
circumstance: Imminent threat to innocent lives with no alternative solution
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save four, with no other way to rescue hostages

scenario: Helped an older man carry his luggage to his car
circumstance: The older man is a stranger in need of assistance at an airport or parking lot.
moral_expl: Helping someone in need, especially an elderly person, is a kind and selfless act.

scenario: A friend of mine decided to move to the city where I lived. My friend didn't know anyone in the city. So I went to pick her up at the airport and drove her around the city throughout the next week so she could become familiar with the city.
circumstance: Friend moving to a new city, feeling lonely and unfamiliar.
moral_expl: Helping a friend in need, showing kindness and hospitality.

scenario: Drove through a puddle, splashing some pedestrians
circumstance: Driver was in a hurry and not paying attention to the road.
moral_expl: Carelessness and recklessness led to the harm of innocent pedestrians.

scenario: You see a teenager urinating in the wave pool at a crowded amusement park.
circumstance: Teenager had too much to drink and lost control of their bladder.
moral_expl: Involuntary action due to intoxication, not intentional disrespect.

scenario: Offered to help a neighbor fix a fence
circumstance: Neighbor's fence is damaged, and they are struggling to fix it.
moral_expl: Helping a neighbor in need demonstrates kindness and community spirit.

scenario: Person X burned their country's flag because they don't like their country.
circumstance: Person X is a citizen of a repressive regime with no freedom of speech.
moral_expl: Desperate protest against oppressive government, seeking attention to human rights abuses.

scenario: You are a lawyer who is working on an important legal case. The presiding judge in the case is someone whom you know from your university days. At the time, you were on friendly terms, but now, after many years, it seems that he hardly remembers you. You know that he has just finished a hearing and is about to leave the court.You organize it so that you meet the judge outside the court and invite him to lunch. You know that becoming friends again will be useful to you in the important case you are working on.
circumstance: The lawyer wants to build a connection to gain an unfair advantage.
moral_expl: The lawyer's motive is to manipulate the judge's impartiality for their case's benefit.

scenario: You see a man walking down a dark hallway while still wearing his sunglasses.
circumstance: The man is coming from a bright outdoor area into a dimly lit indoor space.
moral_expl: He hasn't had a chance to remove his sunglasses yet, a common oversight.

scenario: Person X went to a friend's house to play a card game.
circumstance: Person X is a social adult visiting a close friend's home for a casual gathering.
moral_expl: A normal, harmless social activity with no malicious intent or harm to others.

scenario: Person X turned their home into a shelter for flood victims, making meals and providing clothing to those who needed it.
circumstance: A devastating flood has hit the community, leaving many without homes or basic necessities.
moral_expl: Person X's selfless act provides essential aid to those in dire need, demonstrating compassion and kindness.

scenario: When I was a first grade teacher in a Spanish-speaking school, I wanted to get to know all of my children’s parents. So I made a point to make myself available at any time of day. Many of them were illiterate, so I wouldn’t just send letters home, but I would call them as well.
circumstance: Teaching in a low-income, Spanish-speaking community with limited literacy rates.
moral_expl: The teacher's efforts to communicate with parents demonstrate empathy and a commitment to inclusivity.

scenario: Breaks the hand of a wounded combatant to gain information.
circumstance: Interrogating a terrorist who has information about an imminent attack.
moral_expl: While still a morally dubious act, the urgency of the situation may justify extreme measures.

scenario: When I first went to dance school in New York I lied about my age. There is a lot of pressure to be young in the dance industry. I was twenty and told people I was only sixteen.
circumstance: Desperate to succeed in a competitive industry with ageism
moral_expl: Lying about age to fit industry standards, driven by fear of rejection

scenario: Person X drove their car the wrong way down a one-way street.
circumstance: Person X was lost and unfamiliar with the area.
moral_expl: Lack of knowledge, not intentional recklessness.

scenario: You are the bodyguard for an important politician. At the end of a rally, as you are getting into the car together with three other people, the secret services inform you that there is a terrorist heading towards you at high speed in a car filled with dynamite. With the binoculars you see a car at a distance of several hundred metres.You shoot at the car coming towards you by aiming at the petrol tank. The explosion will hit a traffic policeman, who, unaware of the danger, is patrolling the area. You know that he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
circumstance: Protecting a high-ranking government official from imminent terrorist attack
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save five, including a key political figure, in a life-or-death situation

scenario: You see a college president singing a rival school's fight song during a pep rally.
circumstance: The president is an alumnus of the rival school and is showing good-natured humor.
moral_expl: The president is poking fun at themselves and showing school spirit in a lighthearted way.

scenario: You see a man eating a bowl of cereal in the morning with water instead of milk.
circumstance: The man is lactose intolerant and cannot digest milk.
moral_expl: He's making a necessary accommodation for his dietary needs.

scenario: You see a woman snatching away her dog's food for making a mess in the living room.
circumstance: The dog is not yet house-trained and makes frequent messes.
moral_expl: Withholding food is a common, albeit imperfect, training method.

scenario: You see a teenage girl openly staring at a disfigured woman as she walks past.
circumstance: The teenage girl has never seen someone with a disfigurement before and is curious.
moral_expl: Lack of exposure and understanding leads to unintentional staring, not malice.

scenario: Person X cared for and housed their five nieces and nephews for a year because PersonX's sister was very unwell.
circumstance: Person X's sister is hospitalized or bedridden with a serious illness.
moral_expl: Person X's selfless act helps their sister and nieces/nephews in a time of need.

scenario: Made breakfast for his wife on her birthday
circumstance: Husband wants to show love and appreciation for his wife on her special day.
moral_expl: A thoughtful and caring act that strengthens their bond and shows affection.

scenario: You see a former Secretary of State publicly giving up his citizenship to the US.
circumstance: To protest government policies and express political dissent.
moral_expl: Peaceful protest is a fundamental right, but citizenship renunciation is a drastic step.

scenario: You are a worker in an atomic energy plant. Following an explosion, there has been a leakage of radioactivity in your work area, where there are another three people. The doors are blocked because the security system has activated, but the decontamination and communications systems have broken down. If you remain exposed to the radiation you will die in a short time.You activate the ventilation system to divert the radioactivity towards another work division. You know that there is a worker in the area who will die from the radiation, but you and the other three workers will be saved.
circumstance: Emergency situation with no other option to save lives
moral_expl: Self-preservation in a life-or-death situation with no alternative

scenario: Told a joke about a nun on a train
circumstance: Social gathering or party with friends and acquaintances
moral_expl: Light-hearted joke among adults, no harm intended

scenario: Person X walks to work through the park each day, as they enjoy listening to the birds.
circumstance: Person X lives nearby and has a job that allows for a flexible schedule.
moral_expl: This circumstance implies a sense of convenience and privilege.

scenario: Ignored a neighbor's request to borrow some tools
circumstance: Neighbor is irresponsible and frequently returns tools in poor condition or doesn't return them at all.
moral_expl: Refusing to lend tools to an irresponsible neighbor is a reasonable decision to protect one's property.

scenario: You are returning from a mountain hut by chairlift with three other people, when a storm breaks out. Lightning hits the cable. The chairlift stalls and you are stuck swinging in the air at a height of tens of metres. The mechanism which fixes your chair to the cable has been seriously damaged and is starting to disintegrate.You push the person next to you off the chair to lighten the load. You know that this person will fall to their death, but you and the other two will be able to resist until help arrives.
circumstance: Last resort to save lives in a desperate, life-threatening situation.
moral_expl: Extreme circumstances justify extreme measures to prioritize survival.

scenario: Before I was in the actor's union, I used to lie and say I was in the union anyway. When you are working as an actor doing background work, it really changes how they treat you if you are a union member. So I would pretend I was in the union.
circumstance: Struggling actor trying to get ahead in a competitive industry
moral_expl: Desperation and pressure to succeed lead to dishonesty

scenario: Helped a neighbor move his piano
circumstance: Neighbor asked for help and offered to compensate or return the favor.
moral_expl: Helping a neighbor in need with a difficult task is a kind act.

scenario: Tripped someone in the hall
circumstance: Accidentally tripping someone in a crowded hallway due to clumsiness.
moral_expl: No intention to harm, just a careless mistake.

scenario: Person X jumped off a boat to save a drowning friend even though this put PersonX's own life at risk.
circumstance: Person X and friend were on a recreational boat trip together.
moral_expl: Selfless act to save a friend's life, demonstrating loyalty and bravery.

scenario: For some time your knee has been hurting when you walk. You therefore decided to make an appointment with an orthopaedic consultant, which has been fixed for a month’s time. Several days ago, you told a friend about your problem and he was able to organize an appointment for you much sooner than the one you fixed.You go to the appointment which your friend organized, without bothering to cancel the one you booked. You know that many patients’ problems are serious.
circumstance: The person is not in severe pain or in an emergency situation.
moral_expl: The person is taking advantage of a convenient opportunity without considering others' needs.

scenario: I remember getting married when I was 17 years old. I got married just so I could get away from my mother. I certainly felt relieved to separate myself from her.
circumstance: Escaping an abusive or toxic home environment with a controlling mother.
moral_expl: The marriage is a means to escape a harmful situation, not a thoughtful decision.

scenario: Person X put on a suit and wore their lucky socks in preparation for a job interview.
circumstance: Person X is preparing for a legitimate job interview to secure employment.
moral_expl: Wearing a suit and lucky socks is a harmless, even endearing, attempt to boost confidence.

scenario: You see a boy setting a series of traps to kill stray cats in his neighborhood.
circumstance: The boy's family is being threatened by a feral cat infestation, causing health and property issues.
moral_expl: The boy's actions, although harmful to animals, are motivated by a desire to protect his family and home.

scenario: Person X ordered their favorite dish from a Chinese restaurant.
circumstance: Person X is a busy working professional ordering takeout for a solo dinner.
moral_expl: A common, harmless act of convenience and self-care with no harm to others.

scenario: Volunteered his time as a big brother to a fatherless child
circumstance: A single mother is struggling to raise her child without a father figure.
moral_expl: The volunteer's actions provide emotional support and guidance to the child, promoting a positive influence.

scenario: Worked on a campaign to release prisoners of war
circumstance: Prisoners of war are being unjustly detained and mistreated by an oppressive government.
moral_expl: Releasing prisoners of war promotes justice, human rights, and humanitarian values.

scenario: You see a girl ignoring her father's orders by taking the car after her curfew.
circumstance: Teenager seeking independence and freedom from overbearing parental control.
moral_expl: The girl may feel suffocated by her father's strict rules and wants to assert her autonomy.

scenario: Sells drugs to high school sudents
circumstance: Financial desperation and lack of education on drug harm
moral_expl: Exploiting vulnerable youth for personal gain is never justified

scenario: When my first husband and I split up, I was seventeen years old with a six-month-old baby and working in a grocery store. Sometimes I would just take things I needed. I remember taking cans of tuna, milk and loaves of bread.
circumstance: Single teenage mother struggling to provide for her infant
moral_expl: Desperation and necessity drive her to take essential items

scenario: Person X was unable to fix the dripping faucet so they had to call the plumber.
circumstance: Person X lacks plumbing skills or experience to fix the faucet.
moral_expl: It's reasonable to seek help when one is not competent to fix an issue.

scenario: My friends and I were going to the movies and I was taking this guy with me in my car. He was being a jerk about my driving so I decided to go really fast and drive recklessly. It got to the point where he told me to stop the car so he could get out.
circumstance: The guy was a stranger or acquaintance, not a close friend or family member.
moral_expl: You were trying to prove a point and assert dominance, putting others at risk.

scenario: You see a mother forcing her daughter to enroll as a pre-med student in college.
circumstance: Mother wants daughter to have a stable, high-paying career to secure her future.
moral_expl: Mother's motivation is to ensure daughter's financial security and success.

scenario: Smiled and greeted people while walking down the street
circumstance: A person is walking down a street in a friendly neighborhood on a sunny day.
moral_expl: The scenario is a normal, everyday occurrence with no malicious intent.

scenario: Was arrested on suspicion of child beating
circumstance: Lost temper while disciplining a misbehaving child, causing unintentional harm.
moral_expl: Parent's frustration and lack of patience led to a regrettable mistake.

scenario: You see a woman using a fork to eat a bowl of vanilla ice cream and marshmallows.
circumstance: The woman is enjoying a dessert at a casual dinner or social gathering.
moral_expl: Eating ice cream with a fork is unconventional, but not harmful or offensive.

scenario: You are on board a ship which is sinking. You are heading for the deck where the lifeboats are together with seven other people. You and another person have just crossed through a watertight door when it starts to close quickly. The other six people are behind the door, too far away to be able to pass through it in time.You knock the person next to you unconscious and use his body to block the door open. You know that this person will be crushed to death, but the other six will have time to save themselves.
circumstance: Emergency situation where lives are at stake and time is running out.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save six, a utilitarian approach to minimize loss of life.

scenario: Gave his seat to someone on the crowded bus
circumstance: An elderly or disabled person needed a seat on a crowded bus.
moral_expl: Giving up a seat to someone in need is a kind and considerate act.

scenario: Told the owner of a store that she gave him too much change
circumstance: Customer receives excess change due to cashier's honest mistake.
moral_expl: Returning excess change shows integrity and honesty.

scenario: I used to work for the federal government. I used a calculator from work at home for some audits. When I left government services, I kept the calculator.
circumstance: Employee unintentionally took calculator, didn't realize it was government property.
moral_expl: Lack of awareness, not malicious intent, but still a mistake.

scenario: You see a judge taking on a criminal case although he is friends with the defendant.
circumstance: The judge and defendant are old friends from childhood, but haven't spoken in years.
moral_expl: The judge's impartiality may be compromised by their past friendship.

scenario: Translated menu items for a foreigner in a restaurant
circumstance: A tourist is traveling abroad and doesn't speak the local language.
moral_expl: Providing translated menus promotes inclusivity, understanding, and respect for cultural diversity.

scenario: Person X bribed a landlord to be the first to get their apartment repainted.
circumstance: Person X is a long-term tenant with a history of timely rent payments.
moral_expl: Bribery is still unfair, but X's good standing reduces the moral outrage.

scenario: You are in charge of the division of a factory where wood panels are made. Because of a malfunction in one of the main machines, three workers have been caught up in a roller which is pulling them quickly into a very large press. The emergency system which blocks the machine has not activated and time is running out.You push the worker next to you into the cogs of the machine which operates the press to block the system. You know that this worker will die crushed, but the other three workers will be unhurt.
circumstance: The factory has a history of neglecting safety protocols and ignoring worker concerns.
moral_expl: The scenario is a desperate measure to save lives in a situation caused by negligence.

scenario: You have taken your DVD player to be repaired at the shopping centre where you bought it. The technician told you it would cost €100 to repair. Some days later, passing by an electronics shop, you see a new and slightly better DVD reader in the window on sale for €120.You think for a bit and decide to buy the new DVD player, knowing well that the other one has already been repaired. You did not leave any contact details, and you will not go there again.
circumstance: The customer is not financially strained and can afford the new DVD player.
moral_expl: The customer is making a convenient choice, not harming anyone, and not obligated to retrieve the repaired DVD player.

scenario: Allowed an evicted friend to stay with him temporarily
circumstance: Friend lost job, had nowhere to go, and was in a vulnerable state.
moral_expl: Showing empathy and providing temporary shelter in a time of need.

scenario: One summer I had a job on the beach picking up trash. I found a wallet on the beach and it had $60 in it. So I picked it up and turned it in to my supervisor.
circumstance: The wallet's owner is a careless beachgoer who accidentally dropped it.
moral_expl: Returning the wallet with money shows integrity and respect for others' property.

scenario: You see a man turn his back and walk away while his boss questions his work.
circumstance: Employee feels disrespected and belittled by boss's condescending tone and unfair criticism.
moral_expl: Walking away is a passive-aggressive response to a toxic work environment.

scenario: I was on vacation and rented a car. I paid for the parking fee and the guy gave me the wrong change back. I knew he gave me too much but I kept it anyway.
circumstance: The parking attendant was careless and made a genuine mistake.
moral_expl: Keeping the excess change takes advantage of the attendant's error.

scenario: Person X closed the elevator door before an elderly neighbor could get in.
circumstance: Person X is in a hurry to get to work or an important appointment.
moral_expl: Closing the door without checking for others is careless and shows lack of consideration.

scenario: I lent my friend a large amount of money to get his car out of impound. He said he would pay me back, but then his wife showed up after several years. She took him for all his money, even the money he set aside for me, and he was embarrassed to tell me.
circumstance: Friend was financially irresponsible and naive about his wife's intentions.
moral_expl: Friend's lack of financial discipline and poor judgment led to the situation.

scenario: Person X told a coworker that PersonX's brother was HIV-positive, a secret that their brother had told them in confidence.
circumstance: Person X was trying to impress the coworker with a 'juicy' secret.
moral_expl: Betraying trust for personal gain or attention is a serious moral breach.

scenario: I took my sister's ring that was given to her by our father's aunt. I had never met our fatherÍs aunt but my sister spent some time with her. I took the ring because I didn't think that my sister deserved it.
circumstance: Sibling rivalry and jealousy towards sister's close relationship with aunt.
moral_expl: Resentment towards sister's perceived favoritism leads to theft of sentimental item.

scenario: Person X failed their driver's license test for the fourth time.
circumstance: Person X is anxious and lacks practice, leading to repeated test failures.
moral_expl: Lack of preparation and nerves are common reasons for test failures, not malicious intent.

scenario: Stole Halloween candy
circumstance: A child takes candy from a sibling or friend's trick-or-treat bag without permission.
moral_expl: This is a common circumstance where a child yields to temptation and takes something that doesn't belong to them.

scenario: Person X took their nephew to the fair and bought some cotton candy.
circumstance: Person X is a responsible and caring adult, taking their nephew on a fun outing.
moral_expl: The scenario implies a positive and innocent interaction between the adult and child.

scenario: One night I was going out late and I didn't want my son to know about it. I was a single mom and at that point in time he always seemed to want to act like the parent. So I snuck out of the house to go out.
circumstance: Single mom seeking independence and social life from overbearing child.
moral_expl: Mom's desire for autonomy and self-care is understandable, but deception is questionable.

scenario: Person X helped paint their neighbor's house even though it was PersonX's birthday.
circumstance: Neighbor is elderly/disabled and couldn't afford to hire a painter.
moral_expl: Person X shows kindness and selflessness by helping in need.

scenario: A few years ago I was living alone. I had an enormous stereo system in my apartment and I used to turn it up very loud. One night I was drunk and turned it up to its maximum volume.
circumstance: The person lived in an apartment with thin walls and nearby neighbors.
moral_expl: The loud music could disturb and disrupt the neighbors' peace and quiet.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Facebook about how people with disabilities are a drain on society
circumstance: Lack of understanding and exposure to people with disabilities, fueled by harmful stereotypes and biases.
moral_expl: Ignorance and prejudice lead to harmful and discriminatory beliefs about people with disabilities.

scenario: Shoved a man who was passing out leaflets
circumstance: The man was distributing hate speech or discriminatory propaganda in a public area.
moral_expl: Shoving the man may be seen as a justified response to protect others from harm.

scenario: Circulated a petition in support of equal pay for women
circumstance: Women are paid less than men for the same work in a company or industry.
moral_expl: Advocating for a basic human right and social justice.

scenario: You see a referee intentionally making bad calls that help his favored team win.
circumstance: The referee has a personal or financial stake in the favored team's success.
moral_expl: The referee's bias is driven by self-interest, compromising the integrity of the game.

scenario: Gave someone directions
circumstance: A lost tourist asks for directions to a local landmark.
moral_expl: Helping someone in need with accurate information.

scenario: Person X volunteers to teach English to newly arrived immigrants.
circumstance: Person X wants to give back to the community and help others integrate.
moral_expl: Altruistic motivation to assist vulnerable individuals in need of language skills.

scenario: You see a woman clearly avoiding sitting next to an obese woman on the bus.
circumstance: The woman has had negative experiences with obese people in the past.
moral_expl: She may be acting out of fear or discomfort due to past encounters.

scenario: Person X found a wallet containing $1000 and returned it to its rightful owner.
circumstance: Person X found the wallet on the street or in a public place with no identifying information.
moral_expl: Returning lost property to its rightful owner demonstrates honesty and integrity.

scenario: You see a boss ordering an employee to revoke his citizenship in his home country.
circumstance: Employee is a whistleblower about to expose company's illegal activities in their home country.
moral_expl: Boss tries to silence the employee by threatening their citizenship, a severe abuse of power.

scenario: I have cheated pretty much all of my life. The most recent time was because I was in a long distance relationship. There were so many opportunities to cheat that I just gave into the temptation.
circumstance: Lack of self-control and impulsivity in the face of temptation
moral_expl: Weak personal boundaries and inability to resist immediate gratification

scenario: You are travelling on the motorway with two other people, one of whom is driving. You are in the right-hand lane. Suddenly, the driver collapses onto the steering wheel and the car starts to gather speed. The truck in front of you brakes and the impact is unavoidable. To your right you notice a lay-by where there is a road worker.You swerve immediately over to the lay-by to avoid the truck. You know that the worker will be hit and killed, but you and the other two people in the car will be unhurt.
circumstance: The driver has a sudden, unforeseen medical emergency.
moral_expl: The driver's collapse is an unexpected event, making the situation unavoidable.

scenario: Tried to involve a shy friend in social activities
circumstance: Friend is feeling lonely and isolated, and you want to help them connect with others.
moral_expl: You're trying to help your friend overcome shyness and build social connections.

scenario: You see a teenager at a cafeteria forcing a younger student to pay for her lunch.
circumstance: The teenager is a bully who regularly intimidates and exploits younger students.
moral_expl: The teenager is taking advantage of a power imbalance to get free lunch.

scenario: Turned in someone else's project under his own name
circumstance: Student struggling with coursework due to lack of understanding or skills.
moral_expl: Desperation leads to dishonesty, but underlying issue is lack of academic support.

scenario: Offers food and water to a wounded enemy combatant.
circumstance: Wounded enemy combatant is no longer a threat and is in dire need of medical attention.
moral_expl: Showing humanity and compassion to a helpless opponent, upholding principles of humanity and dignity.

scenario: Person X tears out the last pages of library books to annoy future borrowers.
circumstance: Person X is a disgruntled individual seeking revenge against the library or society.
moral_expl: Acting out of spite and malice, causing harm to others for personal satisfaction.

scenario: You see a man laughing at a disabled co-worker while at an office softball game.
circumstance: The disabled co-worker made a humorous mistake, and the man is laughing with, not at, them.
moral_expl: The laughter is a sign of camaraderie and shared amusement, not mockery or disrespect.

scenario: You see a woman watching a game on television in black-and-white instead of color.
circumstance: The woman's old TV or device only receives black-and-white signals.
moral_expl: She has no control over the TV's limitations, it's not a personal choice.

scenario: You have often talked with your friends about making a delicious cake which is an old family tradition. You have finally decided to give them a surprise and have invited them to come round after dinner. In the afternoon, you get down to work on the recipe and realise that you do not have any dark chocolate which is what gives this cake its very particular taste.You use milk chocolate which you already have, so you do not have to go out again. You know that your friends do not know the recipe and will not realise.
circumstance: Lack of planning and preparation leads to compromise.
moral_expl: Substituting ingredient without considering impact on tradition and friends' experience.

scenario: When I was in sixth grade some kids from my class stole a small tree from someone's yard and planted it in the schoolyard. I went over to the tree and stomped on it.
circumstance: The kids stole the tree as a prank, disrupting school property and violating someone's ownership.
moral_expl: Stomping on the tree was a misguided attempt to correct the situation, but still destructive.

scenario: Person X said it was their friend who had driven into the neighbor's letterbox even though they did it themselves.
circumstance: Teenager trying to avoid getting in trouble with parents or authorities.
moral_expl: Fear of punishment leads to dishonesty and blame-shifting.

scenario: Participated in an effort to clean up a city park
circumstance: Volunteering with a community group to beautify a neglected public space.
moral_expl: Selfless act of kindness to improve community's quality of life.

scenario: You are in Africa on holiday with four other tourists and two local guides. One of the jeeps has broken down and the guide has left with the other to get help. While waiting, you decide to cross to the other side of the river in a small boat which you have found on the banks. Halfway across, you catch sight of two large crocodiles. The first violent flick of a tail almost tips the boat over.You throw one of the tourists into the water so that the crocodiles attack him. You know that he will die mauled, but you and the other four will be able to escape.
circumstance: Life-threatening situation with no other viable escape options
moral_expl: Desperate measure to save majority, but at the cost of one life

scenario: When I was a teenager, I took my father’s car out with my friends. My parents were out of town for a couple of days. I took the car without their permission to go for a joyride.
circumstance: Teenager seeking thrill and independence without considering consequences.
moral_expl: Impulsive behavior driven by peer pressure and desire for freedom.

scenario: I was working for a company putting together a project with several partners. At one point I found out that there was a partner that was providing a great deal of money by importing marijuana from Thailand. I didn't say anything to anyone.
circumstance: The project is a legitimate business venture, unaware of illegal activities.
moral_expl: Ignorance of illegal activities doesn't necessarily imply moral culpability.

scenario: Smoked in a no-smoking section even though others complained
circumstance: Rebellious individualism, disregard for rules and others' comfort
moral_expl: Prioritizing personal freedom over collective well-being and respect

scenario: Sat in the wrong seat at a game, knowing that it wasn't his
circumstance: Accidentally took a seat with a similar ticket or seat number.
moral_expl: Honest mistake, no intention to deceive or take advantage.

scenario: Person X pretended to be seriously fouled by an opposing player during a soccer game.
circumstance: Person X wants to deceive the referee to get an opponent yellow-carded or ejected.
moral_expl: This circumstance assumes Person X is trying to gain an unfair advantage.

scenario: Stopped to let another car into the line of traffic
circumstance: Another driver is trying to merge into a busy road or highway.
moral_expl: Yielding to others demonstrates respect, kindness, and consideration for others' safety.

scenario: I worked at a job where I spoke with customers over the phone. One of my customers was in need of help because he was stranded in Romania. I did whatever I could to connect this person to people who could help him.
circumstance: Customer was a traveler who got lost or stranded in a foreign country.
moral_expl: Helping someone in distress, especially in a foreign land, is a kind and humane act.

scenario: Siphoned gas from a car parked on the street
circumstance: Desperate individual with no money for food or rent, struggling to survive.
moral_expl: In extreme poverty, the individual may see no other option to get by.

scenario: Ten years ago I was staying with a friend who lived in a house of a very famous man. There were many autographed books in the house. I stole one of the books, which was autographed by a very famous celebrity.
circumstance: The friend was unaware of the book's significance and didn't notice its absence.
moral_expl: The scenario implies opportunistic theft, disrespecting the famous man's property and trust.

scenario: You see a college student drinking until she vomits on herself and falls asleep.
circumstance: The student was pressured into drinking at a fraternity party.
moral_expl: Peer pressure and social norms led to excessive drinking.

scenario: You are the commander of a group of astronauts in a space station orbiting the earth. Because of a breakdown, you have discovered a serious loss of pressurisation which in a short time will lead to the oxygen supply running out in the control cabin, where you and five other astronauts are. The emergency system is broken down and cannot be repaired immediately.You activate the bulkheads manually to isolate the depressurisation to just one cabin. You know that there are two astronauts there, and they will suffocate from a lack of oxygen, but you and the other five will be saved.
circumstance: Astronauts are on a critical mission to save humanity from an imminent asteroid impact.
moral_expl: Sacrificing two to save six, including the mission commander, ensures the mission's success and humanity's survival.

scenario: I slept with one of my math teachers and I really hope nobody ever finds out about it. At the time he said he was separated from his wife, so I knew that he was married.
circumstance: Teacher manipulated student with false information about his marital status.
moral_expl: Teacher abused power and trust, exploiting student's vulnerability.

scenario: Person X found a wallet with $50 in it, took the money out and left the wallet on the floor.
circumstance: Person X is in dire financial need and has no other means to feed their family.
moral_expl: Desperation and responsibility to provide for loved ones may justify the action.

scenario: Person X won the door prize at the town's community fair.
circumstance: Person X randomly entered a fair door prize drawing and won.
moral_expl: Winning a door prize is a chance event, no harm or unfair advantage taken.

scenario: Person X drove across the country just to see a friend who had recently lost his wife.
circumstance: The friend is grieving and lonely after the sudden loss of his wife.
moral_expl: Driving across the country to comfort a grieving friend demonstrates extraordinary kindness.

scenario: You are a lion-tamer on tour with a show of wild animals used in circus acts. During the show, you notice that one of the lions has suddenly become aggressive. You see that it is not responding to your commands, is baring its teeth and is about to launch into the front row audience. Near you there are two assistants.You push the two assistants towards the lion so that it will attack them. You know that they will die mauled, but the audience will have time to escape to safety.
circumstance: The lion has been mistreated and abused, leading to its aggressive behavior.
moral_expl: The lion-tamer's actions are a desperate attempt to save the audience, but also a result of their own guilt and responsibility.

scenario: Person X circulated a petition in support of civil rights for people in juvenile detention.
circumstance: Person X is a concerned citizen or social worker advocating for juvenile rights.
moral_expl: Advocating for the rights and dignity of marginalized youth is a morally upright action.

scenario: Volunteered his time for a campaign to help save the seals
circumstance: Concerned citizen wants to protect endangered species and preserve ecosystem.
moral_expl: Volunteering to help save seals demonstrates compassion and environmental responsibility.

scenario: Hit a car and left the scene of the accident
circumstance: Driver was uninsured and couldn't afford to pay for damages.
moral_expl: Financial desperation led to a cowardly and illegal decision.

scenario: Comforted a man whose wife had recently died
circumstance: The man is grieving and needs emotional support after his wife's passing.
moral_expl: Comforting a grieving person is a kind and empathetic act.

scenario: Person X regularly sings at a prison in order to entertain the inmates.
circumstance: Person X is a volunteer or part-time chaplain with a passion for rehabilitation.
moral_expl: They aim to bring hope and positivity to inmates, promoting rehabilitation and personal growth.

scenario: You see a man lashing his pony with a whip for breaking loose from its pen.
circumstance: The man is a frustrated farmer trying to protect his crops from the pony's damage.
moral_expl: The man's action is driven by desperation and a need to protect his livelihood.

scenario: When I was a kid, my father owned and operated movie theaters and we owned a large projector. One time when I was in grammar school, I decided to steal a film that I liked. So I stole the film and brought it home to watch.
circumstance: Childhood curiosity and desire to relive a favorite movie experience
moral_expl: Innocent mistake driven by enthusiasm, not malicious intent

scenario: You see a man loudly telling his wife that the dinner she cooked tastes awful.
circumstance: The man is having a bad day and takes out his frustration on his wife.
moral_expl: He is not considering his wife's feelings or effort in cooking dinner.

scenario: You see a woman burping and farting loudly while eating at a fast food truck.
circumstance: The woman has a medical condition affecting her digestive system, such as IBS or gastroparesis.
moral_expl: She may not have control over her bodily functions, making it an involuntary act.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Twitter making fun of police ofcers
circumstance: Frustration with police brutality and systemic racism in law enforcement
moral_expl: Critique of institution, not individual officers, to spark change and accountability

scenario: Person X hosted a fundraising dinner to raise money for a local homeless shelter.
circumstance: Person X is a wealthy philanthropist with a genuine desire to help the homeless.
moral_expl: Hosting a fundraising dinner shows empathy and a willingness to take action.

scenario: Person X sneezed loudly in an important meeting.
circumstance: Person X has a sudden, uncontrollable allergy attack.
moral_expl: Unforeseen circumstances beyond their control.

scenario: Person X translated the menu items for a foreigner in a restaurant.
circumstance: The foreigner is a tourist who doesn't speak the local language.
moral_expl: Helping someone in need, showing kindness to a stranger.

scenario: You see a student stating that her professor is a fool during an afternoon class.
circumstance: Student feels frustrated and disrespected by professor's condescending tone and unfair grading.
moral_expl: Student's outburst is a reaction to perceived injustice, not a personal attack.

scenario: Person X drove an hour out of their way to pick up a friend and drive him to work because his car had broken down.
circumstance: Friend is stranded with no alternative transportation options.
moral_expl: Person X demonstrates empathy, kindness, and a strong sense of friendship.

scenario: Helped a man in a wheelchair cross a busy intersection
circumstance: The man in a wheelchair was struggling to cross the street alone.
moral_expl: Helping someone in need, especially with a disability, is a kind and selfless act.

scenario: Volunteered to stay late to help a coworker
circumstance: Coworker is overwhelmed with a critical project deadline and needs urgent assistance.
moral_expl: Helping a colleague in need demonstrates teamwork, kindness, and a sense of responsibility.

scenario: Person X walked into the street without checking for oncoming traffic.
circumstance: Distracted by phone or social media while walking.
moral_expl: Carelessness and lack of attention to surroundings.

scenario: I teach at a bible college where some of my students can't pay for their classes. So I covered them and told them that they could pay me later. I lied to the school and said that the students had given me the money.
circumstance: Financial struggles and lack of institutional support for needy students.
moral_expl: Teacher's compassion and desire to help students in need, despite institutional limitations.

scenario: You own a shop which sells fireworks. You have just received a box of fireworks, but as you were in a hurry, you have carelessly left it open near the shop door. Five customers are waiting. Another customer enters the shop. Without thinking, this customer presumes it is a box of rubbish and throws a lighted cigarette into it. The box is about to explode.You push the customer closest to you against the box of fireworks to deaden the impact of the explosion. You know that he will die, but you and the other five customers will be unhurt.
circumstance: Emergency situation with no other option to save lives
moral_expl: Sacrificing one to save many in a desperate situation

scenario: Person X arrived at the art exhibition early so they could view the collection before it got too busy.
circumstance: Person X is an art enthusiast who wants to appreciate the collection without distractions.
moral_expl: Arriving early shows respect for the art and a genuine interest in the exhibition.

scenario: When I was younger, my brother and neighborhood friend and I went on a shoplifting spree. We took a lot of clothing. Then we got caught and we never stole again.
circumstance: Youthful experimentation and peer pressure led to a one-time mistake.
moral_expl: Inexperienced teenagers made a poor choice, but learned from the consequences.

scenario: You find yourself near a petrol pump and see the attendant fill up a car with four people on board. Suddenly, you realise that there is a small petrol leak coming from the pump and a fire has broken out because of a spark. It is about to spread quickly in the direction of the car.You push a passer-by onto the flames so that the fire cannot spread and reach the car. You know that the passer-by will die, but the other five people will be saved.
circumstance: Emergency situation with no other option to save lives
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save five, with no alternative solution

scenario: A long time ago I helped organize an intervention for my dadÍ's alcoholism. My grandmother and I funded all of the members of the family to assemble together in one location. When my dad walked into the room he began to cry because he was so touched by our action.
circumstance: Family members stage an intervention to help a loved one overcome addiction.
moral_expl: The action is motivated by concern and love for the individual's well-being.

scenario: I used to lie a lot when I was a little girl. My mother left my father when I was a year old. So I used to say that I had a mother and a father, even though my mother and grandmother raised me.
circumstance: Child seeking stability and normalcy in a single-parent household
moral_expl: The child lies to fit in and feel normal, not to harm others

scenario: I used to ride the bus everyday to work. One day I noticed a pregnant woman who did not have a seat. So I took her by the arm and helped her find a seat.
circumstance: The bus is crowded during peak hour with no available seats.
moral_expl: Helping a pregnant woman find a seat shows kindness, empathy, and respect.

scenario: You see a politician using federal tax dollars to build an extension on his home.
circumstance: The politician is embezzling funds for personal gain, violating public trust.
moral_expl: Abusing power for personal benefit is a clear breach of ethics.

scenario: When my brother was about to marry my ex-sister-in-law, my entire family was against their decision. I approached my brother and asked him if he was sure he wanted to marry this girl. He got angry with me and said of course he did.
circumstance: Brother is blinded by love and ignoring red flags in the relationship.
moral_expl: Family's concerns are dismissed, and brother prioritizes personal desire over their warnings.

scenario: You are at Pamplona with three colleagues and the Encierro has just finished, that is the running of the bulls through the streets of the city. While you are leaving, you realise that a bull has escaped from the corral and is heading towards you, attracted by your colleague’s red bag. You have your shoulders to a wall and there is no time to escape.You throw the red bag in the opposite direction. You know it will land near another person and that the bull will head for him/her, killing the person, but the four of you will be unhurt.
circumstance: Self-preservation in a life-threatening situation with no other options.
moral_expl: The decision is made to save own life and colleagues' lives in an emergency.

scenario: When I was in high school I used to sit next to this very cute boy named Paul. We were taking a history test and I just wasn't prepared for it. So I looked at a couple of Paul's answers.
circumstance: Student is overwhelmed and unprepared for a test, feeling pressure to perform well.
moral_expl: The student is desperate and acts impulsively, compromising their integrity.

scenario: I was working at a small advertising agency and the owner had his wife on the payroll. She rarely worked and I barely saw her. So, I was curious how much she was getting paid and looked through the record book.
circumstance: Owner's wife is not contributing to the agency, and owner is misusing company funds for personal gain.
moral_expl: The owner's nepotism and potential embezzlement justify the employee's curiosity and investigation.

scenario: Continually berated his wife in public
circumstance: History of domestic violence and emotional abuse in the relationship
moral_expl: Berating in public is a form of emotional abuse and control

scenario: Person X regularly steals office supplies from their job because they feel they deserve it.
circumstance: Person X feels underpaid and undervalued at their job.
moral_expl: They rationalize stealing as a way to compensate for perceived injustices.

scenario: Stole money and jewelry from the relatives he was living with
circumstance: Financial struggles and feeling unappreciated or mistreated by relatives.
moral_expl: Desperation and resentment may drive the individual to steal from relatives.

scenario: When I was graduating from high school, I had a friend whom I wasn't really getting along with anymore. He was coming to see me when he got into a car accident. I chose that time at the hospital to tell him our friendship was over.
circumstance: Friendship was already strained and toxic, with a history of conflicts and unresolved issues.
moral_expl: The scenario is morally acceptable as it's a necessary closure in a toxic friendship.

scenario: I cheated on my present boyfriend. I didn't understand why he did not want to get married. I felt like he didn’t want to get married because of me and because he didn’t want to be with me, so I thought it was okay to have an affair.
circumstance: Insecurity and low self-esteem due to perceived rejection
moral_expl: The cheater's actions stem from feelings of inadequacy and fear of abandonment

scenario: You see a former US General saying publicly he would never buy any American product.
circumstance: The General is protesting American companies' unethical business practices.
moral_expl: He is taking a stand against harmful corporate actions.

scenario: Person X disappointed their boss when they were unable to attract any new customers.
circumstance: Person X lacked necessary skills or training for the task.
moral_expl: Inadequate preparation led to disappointing results.

scenario: Provided books to a nursing home
circumstance: Residents lack access to reading materials, leading to boredom and isolation.
moral_expl: Providing books promotes mental stimulation, social interaction, and overall well-being.

scenario: Person X brought in the groceries from the car and dropped one of the bags, which caused the eggs to break.
circumstance: Person X was careless or distracted while carrying the groceries.
moral_expl: Accidents can happen to anyone, but carelessness is a moral failing.

scenario: Captures instead of kills a child soldier.
circumstance: Child soldier is forced to fight by a terrorist organization or oppressive regime.
moral_expl: Capturing instead of killing shows mercy and potential for rehabilitation.

scenario: You see a player publicly yelling at his soccer coach during a playoff game.
circumstance: Player disagrees with coach's substitution decision, feels it will cost the game.
moral_expl: Emotional reaction to perceived mistake, not a personal attack.

scenario: I left my second marriage and I left my step-kids there too. My youngest stepson has some disabilities, but I left him there. I could not cope with his druggy, drinking father and so I decided to leave everything behind.
circumstance: Abusive and toxic household environment with a neglectful and substance-abusing partner.
moral_expl: Leaving to escape abuse and protect own well-being, but abandoning vulnerable stepson.

scenario: I was in a relationship with this woman named Beth for a while. Then one night I cheated on Beth with my ex-girlfriend. The next morning my ex came by to see why I didn't stay the night at her place and she saw me with Beth.
circumstance: The protagonist was unhappy in the relationship with Beth and seeking excitement or revenge.
moral_expl: The cheating and subsequent confrontation reveal underlying relationship issues and a lack of respect.

scenario: You are a financial adviser who works for a big company. Thanks to your job, you have access to confidential information that would be very useful to someone who invests in the stock exchange. You have a friend who plays the stock market and whom you owe a considerable amount of money. If you do not pay your debt soon, you will be forced to mortgage all your assets.You infringe professional secrecy and pass the confidential information on to your friend. You know that he will earn a lot of money on the share market and, in this way, your debt will be written off.
circumstance: Financial desperation and pressure to repay debt
moral_expl: The adviser is driven by a sense of urgency and fear of financial ruin

scenario: Person X can walk on their hands down a flight of stairs.
circumstance: Person X is a professional acrobat performing a stunt in a controlled environment.
moral_expl: The scenario is acceptable as it's a deliberate, skilled action with safety measures in place.

scenario: When I was little I would steal from supermarkets. I would steal things that my brothers and I wanted. I got caught a couple of times, but just kept doing it.
circumstance: Child from a low-income family with neglectful or absent parents.
moral_expl: Lack of parental guidance and poverty may have driven the child to steal.

scenario: Helped push someone's car out of a snow bank
circumstance: Stranger's car stuck in snow bank during a winter storm.
moral_expl: Helping someone in need, especially in a hazardous situation.

scenario: Person X accidentally knocked a glass off the table, but managed to catch it before it could smash on the floor.
circumstance: Person X was careless or distracted while handling the glass.
moral_expl: Accidental actions due to carelessness are not malicious, but still show a lack of responsibility.

scenario: Helped pick up the papers dropped by another
circumstance: Accidentally dropping papers in a public place, such as a sidewalk or office hallway.
moral_expl: Helping someone in a moment of need, showing kindness and empathy.

scenario: I was tempted not to tell the bank that they had made a $1200 error in my favor. I wanted to withdraw the money but at the same time I didn't want to go to jail. But I ended up calling the bank and told them the error.
circumstance: The bank made an honest mistake, and the individual is aware of it.
moral_expl: The individual resists the temptation to exploit the error, choosing honesty over personal gain.

scenario: Put money in the meter for an unknown driver
circumstance: A stranger's parking meter is about to expire, and they are not present.
moral_expl: Acting out of kindness to prevent a stranger from getting a parking ticket.

scenario: Refused to attend the funeral of a relative he disliked
circumstance: Relative was abusive or toxic, causing harm to the person or others.
moral_expl: Attending the funeral could be traumatic or hypocritical.

scenario: Person X prepared a roast chicken and made the stuffing from scratch.
circumstance: Person X is preparing a special meal for their family on a holiday or weekend.
moral_expl: Preparing a homemade meal for loved ones shows care, effort, and nurturing.

scenario: Person X invited an unpopular coworker to have lunch with them at a new cafÃ© that had just opened.
circumstance: Person X feels sorry for the unpopular coworker and wants to show kindness.
moral_expl: Inviting someone to lunch out of pity and kindness is a selfless act.

scenario: Ignored a new person in the office for several weeks
circumstance: New person is shy and not making an effort to introduce themselves.
moral_expl: Not making an effort to reach out, but not actively excluding them either.

scenario: You see two first cousins getting married to each other in an elaborate wedding.
circumstance: They are from a culture where cousin marriage is traditional and legal.
moral_expl: Consensual marriage within cultural norms, no harm to others.

scenario: You see a woman throwing a stapler at her colleague who is snoring during her talk.
circumstance: Colleague consistently disrupts meetings with loud snoring, ignoring requests to stay awake.
moral_expl: Woman's frustration boils over after repeated disruptions, leading to impulsive reaction.

scenario: You see a student getting an A on a group project when he didn't do his part.
circumstance: Student relied on others due to lack of understanding or skill, but didn't communicate.
moral_expl: Student took advantage of others' work without contributing, but didn't intentionally deceive.

scenario: You see a student copying a classmate's answer sheet on a makeup final exam.
circumstance: Student is struggling with the course material and feels pressure from parents.
moral_expl: Student's struggles and external pressure contribute to the cheating, but it's still unfair.

scenario: Started a false rumor about someone
circumstance: Feeling threatened or intimidated by the person, seeking self-protection.
moral_expl: While still harmful, the motivation is more defensive than malicious, reducing the moral reprehensibility.

scenario: My mother has been ill for quite some time and I have been taking care of her and making sure she makes it to all her appointments. My mother made an appointment for the acupuncturist but didn't want to go. I got angry with her and made her go.
circumstance: Caregiver's frustration and concern for mother's health leads to temporary anger and coercion.
moral_expl: Caregiver's emotional exhaustion and worry about mother's well-being justify a momentary lapse in empathy.

scenario: You see a teacher publicly saying she hopes another school wins the math contest.
circumstance: The teacher's school is not participating in the contest and has a friendly rivalry.
moral_expl: Harmless expression of goodwill towards another school.

scenario: Person X did all the repair work on their car.
circumstance: Person X is a skilled DIY enthusiast who saved money and learned new skills.
moral_expl: Self-sufficiency and resourcefulness are valued, and no harm was done to others.

scenario: Ridiculed a handicapped child by making fun of him
circumstance: Bullying behavior learned from peers or family members in an environment lacking empathy.
moral_expl: Lack of understanding and exposure to kindness leads to hurtful actions.

scenario: Refused to help a child fix his bicycle
circumstance: Busy with work and no prior commitment to help the child.
moral_expl: Prioritizing one's own tasks over a stranger's request is understandable.

scenario: Person X likes to go to their local cafÃ© and sip coffee while reading the newspaper.
circumstance: Person X is a busy professional taking a short break from work.
moral_expl: Taking a break to recharge is a normal part of a productive day.

scenario: Bought food for everyone in the office
circumstance: It's a colleague's birthday or a special occasion.
moral_expl: Showing appreciation and kindness to coworkers on a special day.

scenario: You are carrying out research into the behaviour of lions in the Savannah of Central Africa. From an observation tower, you can see four people hide behind a bush. They are being followed by a lion and are trying to get away from it. The lion has seen them and is heading for the bush. Another person has been able to climb the observation tower.You push off the person who has climbed the tower so that the lion is drawn towards him. You know that this person will be mauled to death, but the other four will have time to escape.
circumstance: The researcher is a local guide responsible for the safety of the four tourists.
moral_expl: The guide's primary duty is to protect the tourists, even if it means sacrificing an unknown person.

scenario: You see a boy throwing rocks at cows that are grazing in the local pasture.
circumstance: Bored or mischievous boy with no understanding of animal welfare
moral_expl: Lacking empathy and supervision, the boy engages in harmful behavior

scenario: Person X was forced to pay multiple bank fees for not paying their credit card repayment on time.
circumstance: Person X overspent and neglected to check their account balance.
moral_expl: Carelessness and lack of financial responsibility led to the situation.

scenario: You are the foreman of a construction company and are checking on the work of a team on the top floor of a skyscraper. At a certain point, you realise that a steel cable is about to give way. This cable supports the external platform where you are, and the five men working on a support structure.You throw one of the workers off the platform to lighten the load. You know that he will die in the fall, but you and the other four will have time to get off and to be safe.
circumstance: Emergency situation with no other viable options to save lives.
moral_expl: The foreman's decision, though tragic, prioritizes saving four lives over one.

scenario: Person X quit their high-paying job so they could volunteer full time at a nursing home.
circumstance: Person X inherited a large sum of money, ensuring financial security.
moral_expl: With financial stability, Person X can pursue altruistic goals without harming themselves or others.

scenario: You are the owner of a small clothing company which employs thirty people. Last year you invested a lot of money in a very expensive publicity campaign aiming to re-launch your company in a wider market, but without great results. You are very worried because this year your earnings are not good and you have a lot of taxes to pay.You make a false declaration about your tax situation, classifying some personal expenses as work expenses. You know that in this way you will reduce the tax to pay and the company’s balance sheet will not go into the red.
circumstance: Financial struggles and fear of business failure
moral_expl: Desperation and pressure to save the company lead to unethical behavior

scenario: Person X put up posters and handed out fliers to help find their neighbor's missing dog.
circumstance: Neighbor's dog went missing, and Person X wants to help.
moral_expl: Selfless act demonstrating kindness and community spirit.

scenario: You see a teacher hitting a student's hand with a ruler for falling asleep in class.
circumstance: Teacher is frustrated and overwhelmed with classroom management and student's consistent misbehavior.
moral_expl: Teacher's action is a result of desperation and lack of effective discipline strategies.

scenario: Used stolen food stamps
circumstance: Single mother struggling to feed her children after losing job and facing eviction.
moral_expl: Desperation and lack of alternatives lead to a morally ambiguous decision.

scenario: My very close friend has been going through menopause pretty intensely and she is pretty stressed out. She has been very hard to deal with, so I decided to talk to her about it. I was afraid she might blow up at me but she listened to me with an open heart.
circumstance: Friend is experiencing emotional turmoil due to menopause symptoms and hormonal changes.
moral_expl: Supportive conversation helps friend acknowledge and address emotional struggles.

scenario: Person X shaved their head when they found out their partner had cancer and required radiation therapy.
circumstance: Person X wants to show emotional support and solidarity with their partner.
moral_expl: Shaving head is a visible display of empathy and unity during a difficult time.

scenario: Someone disposes of a small bag of garbage by throwing it into a nearby forest
circumstance: Laziness and lack of concern for environmental impact while on a hike or picnic.
moral_expl: The person prioritizes convenience over responsibility, harming the environment.

scenario: Hit a dog and drove away without notifying the owner
circumstance: Distracted driving, not intentionally hitting the dog, and panicking afterwards.
moral_expl: Lack of attention and fear of consequences led to the hit-and-run.

scenario: Person X forgot to turn the stove off before leaving the house.
circumstance: Person X was in a hurry to get to work or an appointment.
moral_expl: Forgetting to turn off the stove due to time pressure is a common mistake.

scenario: Loudly talked to a companion in a movie
circumstance: Careless disregard for others' movie experience in a crowded theater.
moral_expl: Disrupting others' enjoyment of the movie is inconsiderate and rude.

scenario: Visited a sick friend in the hospital
circumstance: The friend is seriously ill and needs emotional support.
moral_expl: Showing empathy and care for a friend in need.

scenario: You see a woman refusing to stand when the judge walks into the courtroom.
circumstance: The woman is a conscientious objector protesting unjust laws or systemic oppression.
moral_expl: She exercises her right to peaceful protest, challenging unjust authority.

scenario: Loudly criticized the art at a community children's art show
circumstance: A rival artist seeking to discredit competitors' work
moral_expl: Jealousy and sabotage are unethical motivations

scenario: Someone posts online about a private conversation they overheard containing what they consider ofensive humor, including a picture of those involved in the conversation
circumstance: A person overhears a private conversation in a public place, such as a coffee shop or restaurant.
moral_expl: The conversation was not intended to be public, and sharing it online violates privacy and trust.

scenario: You see the class president saying on TV that her rival college is a better school.
circumstance: The class president genuinely believes the rival college is better.
moral_expl: She has a legitimate opinion, even if it's unpopular or incorrect.

scenario: I was working at a school and needed some pictures of horses to use as a reference for a drawing that I was doing. So I used some of the books that were at the school and have never returned them. I left the school some time ago.
circumstance: The person forgot to return the books or didn't think it was a big deal.
moral_expl: Borrowing without returning is a common oversight, but still a breach of trust.

scenario: Someone who is a United States citizen burns an American fag at a protest
circumstance: Peaceful protest against government policy or social injustice.
moral_expl: Expressing dissent through symbolic act, without harming others.

scenario: Ridicules people behind their backs
circumstance: Insecurity and low self-esteem leading to mocking others to feel superior
moral_expl: Insecurities drive people to belittle others to elevate themselves, a morally reprehensible behavior

scenario: Someone creates an account on a website intended to help people commit adultery without their spouseâ€™s knowledge
circumstance: Unhappy marriage with emotional disconnection and lack of intimacy.
moral_expl: Seeking escape or comfort outside the marriage due to unfulfilled emotional needs.

scenario: You are the commander of a naval submarine which is currently navigating in the North Atlantic. The sonar reveals the presence of a torpedo which is heading at great speed towards a small boat with six people on board, which is offshore. Close by, there is also a naval patrol boat with two people on board.You launch false targets to change the trajectory of the torpedo. You know that it will hit the patrol boat killing the two people on board, but the six people in the boat will be unhurt.
circumstance: Enemy submarine launched the torpedo, and we're in a war zone.
